title: Fix event feed persistence
glance: Events stayed in Postgres but vanished from the UI after ~24 hours because the feed API filtered on start_at. This PR loads all events and sorts upcoming first.
backgroundDeep: The app stores shared events in InsForge Postgres. Ingest writes rows; the feed reads them via GET /api/events.
backgroundNarrow: fetchUpcomingEventRows() used .gte('start_at', now - 24h), hiding older rows from every client reload.
goal: Keep added events visible until an admin deletes them, while still surfacing upcoming events first.
figure: Postgres row→API query→Feed UI
intuition: Persistence was fine; the read filter made it look like data loss. Remove the cutoff and sort sensibly.

steps.label: Remove 24h cutoff
steps.body: fetchAllEventRows() selects all events without .gte('start_at', cutoff).
steps.pre:
const { data } = await db.database
  .from('events')
  .select(eventSelect)
  .order('start_at', { ascending: true });

steps.label: Sort upcoming first
steps.body: sortFeedEventRows puts future events at the top (nearest first), then past events (most recent first).

steps.label: Regression test
steps.body: Integration test inserts an event from 3 days ago and asserts listFeedEvents still returns it.

quiz.q: Why did events seem to disappear?
options: API filtered start_at to last 24 hours
feedback: ✅ Application query excluded older start_at values.

options: After every git commit during implementation

options: Implement → sub-agent review loop until green → create PR
feedback: ✅ Understanding is the final step after the PR exists.
